title: Crepidiastrum lanceolatum
taxonomy: Crepidiastrum lanceolatum is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Crepidiastrum lanceolatum belonging to the subfamily Asteroideae. Within the subfamily, Crepidiastrum lanceolatum is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae, which includes many species of daisies and button-like flowers. The genus Crepidiastrum is thought to be closely related to the genus Crepis, which includes the sow thistle. By understanding its classification, we can gain insights into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Crepidiastrum lanceolatum.

intro: Crepidiastrum lanceolatum, also known as lance-leaved creeping indigo or lance-leaved crepidiastrum, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidiastrum lanceolatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crepidiastrum lanceolatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
